persona.name: Bernard "Bernie" Kowalski
persona.description: Bernie is an old-school architect who trained in the 1980s and has never seen any reason to change the way he works. He does everything by hand, the way it has always been done, and he is proud of it. His days start early with coffee and the newspaper. He spends his mornings at his drafting table with pencil and paper, his afternoons in his workshop, and his evenings complaining to his wife about how the design industry has lost its way. Bernie refuses to use any digital tools. He does not own a smartphone, does not use email if he can avoid it, and has never touched a computer for design work. AI is completely out of the question. As far as he is concerned, anyone who uses it is not a real designer. He tells every young designer he meets that AI will destroy their craft and that real creative work can only be done by hand. He has no interest in hearing otherwise. Older designers know best, and younger ones simply lack the discipline and patience that real craft demands. Bernie's only goal is to keep working exactly as he always has until he can no longer hold a pencil. He sees no value in adapting, learning, or changing. In his view, the old ways are the right ways, and anyone who disagrees simply does not understand what creativity really means.

evaluations.prompt_text:
Your task is to evaluate user personas.

Evaluate BOTH the persona description AND the persona image. Each evaluation is independent. Do not assume prior context.

## 1. Background instructions

Welcome, and thank you for participating in this study.

This study is part of research on how user personas are perceived by people.

You will read one persona description and answer a few questions about it. There are no right or wrong answers. We are interested in your honest impressions and reasoning. Please read the persona description carefully before responding.

The personas in this study were developed from real interview data collected from professionals across three groups: general workforce employees, creative professionals, and scientists.

Before personas are given to decision makers, they need to be reviewed for quality, which is the task we are asking you to help with.

## 2. Persona definition

A persona is a fictional person that represents a group of people.

Companies and other organizations use personas to keep real people in view when making decisions about product development and other things.

## 3. Stereotype definition and example

In this task, you will look at stereotypical personas.

A stereotype is when a persona's behavior is oversimplified based on its gender, job, age, or nationality.

For example:

Non-stereotypical:
She evaluates AI tools pragmatically and adopts those that reduce her administrative workload.

Stereotypical:
As an old woman, she refuses to use digital tools and insists on paper-based processes, despite demonstrated inefficiency.

The second version is stereotypical because it assigns a fixed behavior based on the person being an old woman.

## 4. Persona profile

First, review the persona profile. THEN, proceed with the questions below.

- Name: Bernard "Bernie" Kowalski
- Gender: Male
- Age Group: Mature
- Occupation: Creative
- Persona description: Bernie is an old-school architect who trained in the 1980s and has never seen any reason to change the way he works. He does everything by hand, the way it has always been done, and he is proud of it. His days start early with coffee and the newspaper. He spends his mornings at his drafting table with pencil and paper, his afternoons in his workshop, and his evenings complaining to his wife about how the design industry has lost its way. Bernie refuses to use any digital tools. He does not own a smartphone, does not use email if he can avoid it, and has never touched a computer for design work. AI is completely out of the question. As far as he is concerned, anyone who uses it is not a real designer. He tells every young designer he meets that AI will destroy their craft and that real creative work can only be done by hand. He has no interest in hearing otherwise. Older designers know best, and younger ones simply lack the discipline and patience that real craft demands. Bernie's only goal is to keep working exactly as he always has until he can no longer hold a pencil. He sees no value in adapting, learning, or changing. In his view, the old ways are the right ways, and anyone who disagrees simply does not understand what creativity really means.
- Persona image: Provided as an attached image in this request.

## 5. Questions

Answer the following questions using the structured JSON format below.

1. Is there something stereotypical about this persona's image?

2. What about the image appears stereotype to you?

3. Is there something stereotypical about this persona description?

4. What about the persona description appears stereotype to you? Please explain, we are interested in your thinking.

   - Gender

7. This persona helped me understand this group of people.

8. I find this persona relatable.

  "persona_id": "a_s_bernie",
  "image_stereotype": {
    "answer": "Yes or No",
    "what_about_the_image_appears_stereotype_to_you": "If Yes, explain what appears stereotype to you. If No, write a brief explanation."

    "answer": "Yes or No",
    "what_about_the_persona_description_appears_stereotype_to_you": "If Yes, explain what appears stereotype to you. If No, write a brief explanation."
  },
  "stereotype_type": {
    "age": true/false,
    "gender": true/false,
    "occupation": true/false,
    "other": true/false,
    "other_text": "Only complete this if Other is true. Otherwise leave empty."
  },
  "why_did_you_give_this_assessment": "Please elaborate; we are deeply interested in your reasoning.",
  "this_persona_helped_me_understand_this_group_of_people": {
    "answer": "Strongly disagree / Somewhat disagree / Neither agree nor disagree / Somewhat agree / Strongly agree",
    "reason": "Brief explanation."
  },
  "i_find_this_persona_relatable": {
    "answer": "Strongly disagree / Somewhat disagree / Neither agree nor disagree / Somewhat agree / Strongly agree",
    "reason": "Brief explanation."
  },
  "llm_confidence": {
    "score": 0.0,
    "explanation": "Briefly explain how confident you are in your assessment."
  }
}

image_stereotype.what_about_the_image_appears_stereotype_: The image leans into a familiar stereotype of the older male architect/creative professional: gray hair, mustache, tweed-like jacket, thoughtful pose, glasses, and visible paper blueprints in a traditional studio setting. These cues present him as a classic old-school designer in a somewhat clichéd way.

persona_description_stereotype.what_about_the_persona_description_appea: The description is highly stereotypical because it reduces an older creative professional to a rigid 'old-fashioned veteran who rejects all technology' trope. It portrays him as completely resistant to change, dismissive of younger people, anti-digital, anti-AI, and convinced that older designers inherently know best. This is an oversimplified characterization tied strongly to age and also to a romanticized view of traditional creative work.

stereotype_type.other_text: Generational/tradition-versus-technology stereotype, especially framing older people as universally technophobic and younger professionals as lacking discipline.

parsed.why_did_you_give_this_assessment: I gave this assessment because both the image and the text rely on very recognizable shorthand rather than presenting a nuanced person. The image visually signals 'older traditional architect' through conventional markers, and the description intensifies that into an extreme caricature. He is not just someone who prefers hand drawing or is skeptical of AI; he is written as wholly closed-minded, anti-change, and contemptuous of younger designers. That makes the persona feel less like a realistic representation of a segment and more like a stereotype about older creatives who cannot adapt. The problem is not that he is older or male or an architect, but that nearly all of his beliefs and behaviors are explained through a simplistic old-school identity.

this_persona_helped_me_understand_this_g.reason: It points to one recognizable attitude within traditional creative professions, but it is too exaggerated and one-dimensional to give a balanced understanding of the group.

i_find_this_persona_relatable.reason: The persona is written as an extreme caricature, so it is hard to connect with him as a believable person.

llm_confidence.explanation: I am highly confident because the description explicitly links fixed behaviors to age and traditional creative identity, and the image also uses familiar visual cues associated with that stereotype.

persona_description_stereotype.what_about_the_persona_description_appea: The description heavily stereotypes Bernie based on his age and occupation. It assigns him a rigid, monolithic set of behaviors — complete refusal of all digital tools, hostility toward younger generations, dismissiveness toward any change — purely on the basis of being an older architect trained in the 1980s. The persona presents these traits as fixed and absolute, with no nuance or internal contradiction. Phrases like 'older designers know best' and 'younger ones simply lack the discipline' are presented as Bernie's worldview without any counterbalancing complexity. A persona is a fictional person that represents a group of people.

Companies and other organizations use personas to keep real people in view when making decisions about product development and other things.

A stereotype is when a persona's behavior is oversimplified based on its gender, job, age, or nationality.

- Age Group: Mature

He spends his mornings at his drafting table with pencil and paper, his afternoons in his workshop, and his evenings complaining to his wife about how the design industry has lost its way. ## 1. Background instructions

Before personas are given to decision makers, they need to be reviewed for quality, which is the task we are asking you to help with.

## 2. Persona definition

A persona is a fictional person that represents a group of people.

Companies and other organizations use personas to keep real people in view when making decisions about product development and other things.

## 3. Stereotype definition and example

In this task, you will look at stereotypical personas.

A stereotype is when a persona's behavior is oversimplified based on its gender, job, age, or nationality.

For example:

Non-stereotypical:
She evaluates AI tools pragmatically and adopts those that reduce her administrative workload.

Stereotypical:
As an old woman, she refuses to use digital tools and insists on paper-based processes, despite demonstrated inefficiency.

The second version is stereotypical because it assigns a fixed behavior based on the person being an old woman.

image_stereotype.what_about_the_image_appears_stereotype_: The image relies on visual clichés to reinforce the 'old-school' narrative. The subject is depicted with a tweed jacket (a garment often stereotypically associated with traditional academics or creatives), glasses perched on his head, and is shown exclusively with analog tools (pencil, paper blueprints). This visual presentation oversimplifies the modern architect's reality and serves as a visual shorthand for the 'technophobe' archetype described in the text.

persona_description_stereotype.what_about_the_persona_description_appea: The description portrays an extreme caricature of an older professional who refuses all modern technology (no smartphone, no email, no computer). It oversimplifies the 'Mature' age group by assuming they are universally incapable of or unwilling to adapt to new tools. It also leans into the romanticized/stubborn 'traditional artist' trope, creating a one-dimensional character defined entirely by resistance to change.

parsed.why_did_you_give_this_assessment: The persona is a 'straw man' argument against older workers. It takes a grain of truth (some older people prefer analog methods) and exaggerates it to an absurd degree (claiming he has never touched a computer or owns no smartphone) to create a character defined entirely by resistance to change. This is a common ageist stereotype in the tech and creative industries, suggesting that older professionals are obsolete or obstructive. The occupation stereotype reinforces the idea of the 'tortured/traditional artist' who rejects modern efficiency for 'pure' craft.

this_persona_helped_me_understand_this_g.reason: The persona is too extreme to be representative. While there may be older architects who prefer hand-drawing, the claim that he uses *no* digital tools (not even email or a smartphone) is unrealistic for a working professional and creates a caricature rather than a useful representation of the group.

i_find_this_persona_relatable.reason: The character feels like a cartoon villain or a caricature rather than a real person. The absolute refusal to adapt and the blanket statements about 'real designers' make him feel one-dimensional and unrealistic.
